title: Construct Binary Tree from Inorder and Postorder Traversal
hints: In postorder, the last element is always the root of the current subtree. Find this root in the inorder array to split left and right children.
Use the index of the root in inorder to determine the size of the left subtree. This size helps identify the corresponding slice in postorder for the left child.
Process postorder from right to left. Recursively build the right subtree first, then the left, using a shared pointer to track the current root in postorder.
